hook_type: Personal address + specific stat + recursion in one sentence
tactic: 'You helped build a $234M platform' -> 'now we are raising on it' recursion
primary_text:
You helped build a $234M platform. Now we are raising on it.

You are one of 137,000 investors who put money behind 324 Australian companies on Birchal. Every dollar came from people choosing to own a piece of something they believed in, not from VCs.

Now we are doing the same, opening our own raise.

Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: You helped fund 324 companies. Our turn now.
description: Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live.
_source_label: DRC V4 -- B1 Community-as-builder / Recursion (existing investors)
_v4_changes: Hook rewrite: 'You are one of 137,000 investors who built this platform. Now we are raising on it.' -> 'You helped build a $234M platform. Now we are raising on it.' Adds a number ($234M) into the first 8 words for the specificity signal the V3 first sentence lacked (V3 first sentence 'You are one of 137,000 investors who built this platform.' carried personal address + 137,000 but reads slower). V4 first sentence scores Halbert 5/5 in --primary-text mode: personal address + $234M specificity + named entity, with a tighter recursion beat.

Para 2 keeps the 137,000 / 324 / 'not VCs' proof but moves the community number into the body where it supports rather than competes with the $234M hook. 'choosing to own a piece' retains the load-bearing ownership phrase.

Headline rewrite: 'After 324 raises on Birchal, our turn' -> 'You helped fund 324 companies. Our turn now.' Direct second-person address for the existing-investor list, recursion punch, Halbert 5/5 (V3 headline scored 4).
80 words (V3 B1 was 67; V4 adds the $234M hook specificity and 324-companies detail while staying at the top of the band).
_drc_playbook: 11 Lifecycle Email Promo / Launch (B2C considered, most-aware, active list)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who have made at least one investment on the platform.
_a_pile_signals: recursion (now we are raising on it)
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'you will see the offer'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'you will see the offer the moment it goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
Anti-preferential terms (s 738G): waitlist remains free and equal-access, no preferential framing vs the public offer.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' once; first-person we/our elsewhere; recursion subjects unambiguous (you = the investor, we = the company raising).
